title: practical english usage - Part 255
content: Expressions - Making Sense of Transatlantic English, edited by Orin Hargraves, useful guide to vocabulary differences is Mighty Fine Words and Smashing with very brief information about the words and their meanings. A  very are used for the same idea (BrE lorry = AmE   truck). Here are a few examples, meanings (BrE mad = crazy; AmE   mad = angry). And very often, different words There are very many differences. Sometimes the same word has different 2 vocabulary:       sidewalk/pavement Varieties of English  Section 28 grammar •  319   American and British English look around the church Some of the English (e.g. center/centre). Many verbs which  end in -ize in American English (e.g. color/colour). Some words end in -er in American English and -re in British A  number of words end in -or in American English and -our in British English 3 spelling:     color/colour ten to/of/before/till four                     ten to four ten after/past four (time)                          ten past four on the weekend                                       at the weekend on a team                                               in a team Monday through/to Friday                      Monday to Friday meet with somebody (planned)               meet (with) somebody American English                                  British English Varieties of English  Section 28 grammar «319      American and British English BrE /'fɜ:taɪl/ (rhyming with her tile) fertile  AmE   /'fɜɪrtl/ (rhyming with turtle) British English; some are pronounced with /1/  in American English.
